description_long: This object consists of three large, rectangular electrical transformers arranged side by side, each with prominent cooling fins and metallic housings. The central unit is the largest, featuring a cylindrical conservator tank mounted on top and several bushings with insulator stacks protruding from the upper surface. The two flanking units are smaller, with visible terminal connectors and insulator stacks on their tops, and all three units rest on sturdy metal feet. The surfaces are primarily metallic gray and black, with some copper or brass-colored electrical contacts and connectors. The overall construction is robust, with heavy-duty metal components and visible bolted joints, designed to withstand outdoor urban environments. The object is likely used for stepping down high-voltage electricity for distribution in city infrastructure.
description: A set of three heavy-duty, metallic electrical transformers with cooling fins, insulator stacks, and terminal connectors, arranged side by side. The central unit is the largest and features a cylindrical conservator tank on top.
description_view_0: Three transformers are visible side by side, showing their cooling fins, insulator stacks, and the cylindrical tank on the central unit.
description_view_1: A direct end-on view of the transformers, highlighting the depth and alignment of the units, with the central unit's tank visible at the back.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the transformers, again showing the three units in a row, with the cylindrical tank and insulator stacks clearly visible.
description_view_3: A rear view of the transformers, emphasizing the cooling fins and the arrangement of the insulator stacks and connectors on top.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: There are no traditional crossarms; instead, insulator stacks and terminal connectors are mounted directly on the top surfaces of each transformer in a linear arrangement.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: Power lines are attached to the top of each transformer via insulator stacks, likely supporting a three-phase configuration with parallel routing for each unit.
